content.full_text:
April 26, 1977

Kare Anderson  
Senator Peter Behr's Office  
State Capitol, Room 5053

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND ANALYSIS DIVISION

February 14, 1977 Letter to Senator Behr From Fred Sibley

We are having a terrible time getting together by telephone so I thought I should send over a note concerning the above mentioned letter.

Mr. Sibley is concerned that officeholders who are also designated under many conflict of interest codes have an obligation to file a complete financial disclosure statement at each designated office rather than being able to file one statement in a central office with the record in each of the other offices showing where the original document is on file. The Commission has addressed this problem and concluded that the public should have access to a statement at each agency rather than be required to go to a specified central office in order to secure the information.

If the Senator or Mr. Sibley would like to express their opposition to this advice, I would suggest they write to Commission Chairman Dan Lowenstein recommending an alternative method of filing. The Commission vote was not unanimous on this advice.

Please call me if I can be of additional help.

REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS  
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

bcc: Jeanne P.
